notes: v1.14.127 - UI sweep RULE E (header chrome on a baseline) + B2 (Command Lens ON SHIFT pill placement). (1) B2: the #cc-shiftpill 'ON SHIFT · Xh Ym' pill was position:absolute (top:74px;right:18px), floating over the Command Lens title ('AUS-01 Ready State'). Moved it in-flow to BELOW the description, left-aligned (markup relocated after #cc-lp; CSS now display:inline-flex;margin-top:12px, dropped absolute/top/right/z-index). Browser-verified: pill left=47 == description left=47, sits below the desc; cmd_render show/hide (active deployment) unchanged. (2) RULE E: the left header is a 2-line stack (wordmark + COREWEAVE·site subtitle) while the right (shield + SYS) centers on the tall shield, so the wordmark rode ~10px above the SYS pill. Added body.rd .header-right{position:relative;top:-9px} so the shield/SYS group aligns to the wordmark's line - verified wordmark center y=23 vs SYS center y=24 (delta 1px, was 10px). CSS + markup, redesign-only (#pg-cmd is New-UI; .header-right nudge is body.rd-gated) -> ?legacy=1 byte-identical. node --check x3 + sw.js, brace gate, three-stamp lockstep. DEVICE-VERIFY: Command Lens ON SHIFT pill sits below the description (no overlap with the title); header wordmark + shield + SYS read on one baseline; ?legacy=1 unchanged.
